lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: The city is flourishing, largely thanks to you. There's still some mistrust for the Iorwerth clan, but overall I'd say it's been over a thousand years since our people were doing this well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great to hear. What about you though?

lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: I still mourn Glarial every day, until the end of time a part of me will be missing, never to be replaced.

lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: But I know now that I can't let that impact the elves here. Many of them never even knew their former queen. My grief is mine alone, they need not bear it.

lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: To them, I'm trying my best to be a leader they deserve, to correct the mistakes of the past. Lord Iorwerth believed we were nothing without Seren. We need to prove that we have a future with her gone.

lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: Thankfully I have the other elders to help this time. Ruling is not as hard when you share out the responsiblities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad to hear things are going well.

lines.speaker: Lord Baxtorian Cadarn
lines.text: And we are glad to have you here with us. None of this would have been possible without you.
